substitutes.notes: triple sec is sweeter, slightly less refined
campari more bitter; use a bit less

recipes.name: Daiquiri
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients hard with ice, double strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Margarita
recipes.instructions: Rim glass with salt. Shake tequila, Cointreau, and lime juice with ice. Strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.

recipes.name: Whiskey Sour
recipes.instructions: Dry shake (no ice) to emulsify egg white, then wet shake with ice. Strain into a coupe.

recipes.name: Sidecar
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Gimlet
recipes.instructions: Shake gin and lime cordial (or lime juice + syrup) with ice; strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Cosmopolitan
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a martini glass.

recipes.name: Kamikaze
recipes.instructions: Shake with ice and strain.

recipes.name: Caipirinha
recipes.instructions: Muddle lime wedges and sugar in a rocks glass. Add cachaça and crushed ice; stir.

recipes.name: Gin & Tonic
recipes.instructions: Build gin over ice in a highball glass. Top with tonic water and garnish.

recipes.name: Moscow Mule
recipes.instructions: Build in a copper mug or highball over ice. Top with ginger beer.

recipes.name: Sea Breeze
recipes.instructions: Build in an ice-filled highball glass. Pour vodka, then grapefruit juice, then top with cranberry juice. Garnish with a lime wedge.

recipes.name: Dark 'n Stormy
recipes.instructions: Build dark rum over ice. Top with ginger beer.

recipes.name: Paloma
recipes.instructions: Build tequila and lime juice over ice. Top with grapefruit soda (or fresh juice + soda).

recipes.name: Mojito
recipes.instructions: Muddle mint with sugar and lime juice in a highball. Add rum and crushed ice. Stir and top with soda.

recipes.name: Cuba Libre
recipes.instructions: Build over ice and stir.

recipes.name: Tom Collins
recipes.instructions: Shake gin, lemon, and syrup with ice. Strain into Collins glass over ice and top with soda.

recipes.name: Old Fashioned
recipes.instructions: Stir sugar, bitters, and a splash of water until dissolved. Add bourbon and a large ice cube. Stir to chill and dilute. Express orange twist over the glass.

recipes.name: Sazerac
recipes.instructions: Rinse a chilled rocks glass with absinthe and discard. Stir rye, sugar, and bitters with ice. Strain into the absinthe-rinsed glass. Express lemon peel over.

recipes.name: Dry Martini
recipes.instructions: Stir gin and dry vermouth over ice until very cold. Strain into a chilled martini glass.

recipes.name: Manhattan
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Negroni
recipes.instructions: Stir all three ingredients with ice; strain over a large ice cube in a rocks glass.

recipes.name: Boulevardier
recipes.instructions: Stir with ice; strain over a large rock.

recipes.name: Rob Roy
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Vesper
recipes.instructions: Shake well over ice and strain into a chilled glass. "Shaken, not stirred."

recipes.name: Martinez
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Aperol Spritz
recipes.instructions: Fill a large wine glass with ice. Add prosecco first, then Aperol, then a splash of soda. Stir gently.

recipes.name: Campari Spritz
recipes.instructions: Build in a wine glass with ice.

recipes.name: Americano
recipes.instructions: Build in a highball over ice; top with soda.

recipes.name: Gin Fizz
recipes.instructions: Shake gin, lemon, and syrup hard with ice. Strain into a highball with ice. Top with soda.

recipes.name: Silver Fizz
recipes.instructions: Dry shake with egg white, then wet shake with ice. Strain into a highball and top with soda.

recipes.name: French 75
recipes.instructions: Shake gin, lemon, and syrup with ice; strain into a flute and top with champagne.

recipes.name: Mint Julep
recipes.instructions: Muddle mint gently with syrup in a metal julep cup. Add bourbon and pack with crushed ice. Swizzle until cup frosts.

recipes.name: Brandy Flip
recipes.instructions: Dry shake to emulsify egg, then wet shake with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Mai Tai
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with crushed ice. Pour unstrained into a rocks glass. Garnish heavily.

recipes.name: Pisco Sour
recipes.instructions: Dry shake pisco, lemon, syrup, and egg white. Wet shake with ice. Strain. Dot foam with angostura.

recipes.name: Bloody Mary
recipes.instructions: Roll between two tins (or gently stir) over ice. Strain into a highball with fresh ice.

recipes.name: Corpse Reviver #2

recipes.name: Last Word
recipes.instructions: Shake equal parts with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Espresso Martini
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients hard with ice until foamy. Double strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: White Lady
recipes.instructions: Shake gin, Cointreau, and lemon with ice. Double strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Hemingway Daiquiri
recipes.instructions: Shake well with ice, double strain into a chilled coupe.

recipes.name: Black Russian

recipes.name: White Russian

recipes.name: Grasshopper

recipes.name: Abacaxi Ricaço
recipes.garnish: Cut a straw sized hole in the top of the pineapple shell & replace it as a lid
recipes.instructions: Cut the top off a small pineapple and carefully scoop out the flesh from the base to leave a shell with 12mm (½ inch) thick walls. Place the shell in a freezer to chill. Remove the hard core from the pineapple flesh and discard; roughly chop the remaining flesh, add other ingredients and BLEND with one 12oz scoop of crushed ice. Pour into the pineapple shell and serve with straws. (The flesh of one pineapple blended with the following ingredients will fill at least two shells).

recipes.name: Almond Old Fashioned (tequila based)
recipes.instructions: STIR one shot of tequila with two ice cubes in a glass. Add amaretto, agave syrup, bitters and two more ice cubes. Stir some more then add another two ice cubes and the remaining tequila. Stir lots more so as to melt ice then add more ice. The melting and stirring in of ice cubes is essential to the dilution and taste of the drink.

recipes.name: Brandy Crusta
recipes.garnish: Some merely shove a wide long piece of lemon peel around the inside of the glass rim, leaving the drinker to push the peel down into the glass in order to sip from the drink. Instead I prefer the peel to become a watertight extension to the glass. Find a lemon which fits snugly in the top of a small wine, flute or sour glass. Cut off both ends of the fruit and carefully remove the pulp to leave a barrel-shaped shell of skin. Place in the top of the glass. Wet the edge of the glass and exposed fruit shell with sugar syrup and dip in caster sugar to frost the edge of both peel and glass. Leave for a couple of hours to form a hard crust that helps secure the peel.

recipes.name: Dry Martini - The 'Naked' or 'Direct' Martini
recipes.garnish: Chilled olive on stick or lemon zest twist
recipes.instructions:
1. Place glass and bottle of gin in freezer for at least two hours before making drink.
2. An hour before making drink POUR water into frozen glass, swirl around to coat and place back in freezer. Preferably do this with a little of the water at a time. Continue to swill around during freeing process to coat the sides of the glass.
3. When ready to serve drink, POUR chilled vermouth into icy glass and swirl to coat with vermouth.
4. POUR frozen gin into glass, garnish and serve immediately.

recipes.name: Flaming Ferrari
recipes.garnish: Assistant to help the drinker consume the concoction
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: LAYER the first four ingredients by carefully pouring in order into a Martini glass.
Step 2: In two shot glasses POUR the remaining two ingredients separately.
Step 3: IGNITE the contents of the Martini glass. Give two long straws to the drinker and instruct them to drink the contents of the Martini glass in one go. As they do so, slowly POUR the contents of the two shot glasses into the flaming Martini glass.

recipes.name: Passion Fruit Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Chop passion fruit in half and scoop flesh into the rocks glass and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Raspberry Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add raspberries and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Tropical Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

4 POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer), coconut liqueur and pineapple juice (also chilled) into the shaker base or glass used to muddle and swirl to rinse before pouring into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

5. Use an Ice Tapper to crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Crustas (generic name)
recipes.garnish: Find a lemon which fits into a small wineglass tightly enough to act as a watertight extension to the glass. Cut off both ends of the fruit and carefully remove the pulp to leave a barrel-shaped shell of skin. Place in the top of the glass. Wet the edge of the glass and exposed fruit shell with sugar syrup and dip in caster sugar to frost the edge of both peel and glass. Leave for a couple of hours to form a hard crust.

recipes.name: Benton's Old Fashioned
recipes.instructions:
STIR bourbon and maple syrup in base of mixing glass until syrup dissolves. Add bitters and ice and STIR again. Strain into ice-filled glass.

*To make fat-washed bourbon: Grill 4 slices of bacon to obtain 30ml/1oz of warm fat (originally made using Benton's bacon, hence the name, but any extra-smoky bacon will suffice) and pour this into a 70cl of bourbon (in a 75cl bottle). Leave to infuse at room temperature for a day before placing bottle in a freezer to solidify the fat. Then clarify the bourbon by straining into a clean bottle.

Optionally, separately, POUR champagne into chilled shot glass to serve on the side. Instruct drinker to sip alternately from each glass.

recipes.name: Estrella Porno Martini
recipes.instructions:
1. To make a Estrella Porno Martini you’ll first need to flavour your tequila with a vanilla pod. Simply cut the vanilla pod lengthways to expose the flavoursome seeds inside and drop the whole vanilla pod into your bottle of tequila. Reseal and shake bottle several times a day for at least two days, preferably a week.

2. To make your cocktail, wash and cut passion fruits in half, SCOOP out the seeds and flesh of 3 halves into shaker. (Keep last passion fruit half for garnish). Add next four ingredients (all but champagne), SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. POUR champagne into chilled shot glass to serve on the side. Instruct drinker to sip alternately from each glass.

recipes.name: Blueberry Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add blueberries and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Strawberry & Basil Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:

2. Add 3 bar spoons powdered caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add strawberries and basil, and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Using an Ice Tapper, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add watermelon and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an Ice Tapper and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Pineapple Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add pineapple and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an Ice Tapper and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Caipirinha (no added sugar & low-calorie)
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and slice on half into 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add sweetener and MUDDLE.

3. If using cubed ice straight from the freezer, add chilled water (or optionally add fruit of your choice) and MUDDLE some more. If using watery ice then omit water.

4. When sure muddling has fully extracted fruit juice, POUR the muddled fruit and sweetener into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (the glass used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to mix with other ingredients.

6. If using cubed ice, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Strawberry Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons powdered caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add strawberries and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes using an Ice Tapper and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Bird of Paradise Gin Fizz
recipes.instructions:
SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and slowly pour 2/3rds of the contents of the shaker from a higher than normal height into a chilled empty glass (no ice in glass) while simultaneously pouring soda with your other hand. The cocktail and soda should form a single stream of liquid falling into the glass.

As when pouring a Guinness, to achieve a perfect head on a fizz, the cocktail should be left to settle for at least a minute before topping off with what remains in the shaker. Ideally, during the settling period, the glass should be placed in a glass froster/fridge/freezer. As the final contents of the shaker are poured into the glass so the head should rise like a souffle.

recipes.name: LaMarhattan
recipes.instructions:
> Ice a Martini glass.

>Fill a stainless cocktail shaker with ice:
Add 1.5 oz Maker's Mark,
Add 1 oz Italian sweet vermouth,
Add 0.5 oz Luxardo cherry liqueur,
Add 2 dashes Angostura bitters (yellow cap).

> Shake till its too cold to hold.

> Dump the ice from the Martini glass and place one or two Luxardo maraschino cherries in the glass; on a pick if you wish.

IMPORTANT: no cheap day-glo-red faux maraschino cherries!

> Strain the drink via the shaker's strainer into the glass.

> Sip slowly, but finish while still cold.

>Rinse glass, and repeat as necessary.

recipes.name: Vetiver Whisky Sour
recipes.instructions:
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled coupe glass.

For the Whisky we highly recommend Johnnie Walker Black Label. The slight smokiness and the overall character works best with the wooden tones and the Vetiver aspect of the drink.

For the Vetiver Gris Espuma, use slightly whipped egg whites with a few cl of the vetiver Liqueur, add a pinch of Salt and some kind of cream stiffener. Use two capsules of nitrogen NO2 in your Isi Cream Whipper, shake a few times and let rest in a cool enviroment, laying down, for atleast 30 mins.

recipes.instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a frozen tumbler glass and stir to integrate ground coffee. (Vasilis Kyritsis specifies Colombia coffee as this "gives more acid notes with floral and fruit aftertaste). If you don't have scales to hand then 10 gram of ground coffee has a volume of approx. 30ml in an Easy Jigger
2. Moisten filter paper in a Hario V60 dripper or similar and place over chilled cup. Pour combined ingredients into filter.
3. Patiently wait a couple of minutes while the cocktail drips through the paper and then enjoy.

recipes.name: Chicago Fizz
recipes.instructions:
SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and slowly pour 2/3rds of the contents of the shaker from a higher than normal height into a chilled empty glass (no ice in glass) while simultaneously pouring soda with your other hand. The cocktail and soda should form a single stream of liquid falling into the glass.

As when pouring a Guinness, to achieve a perfect head on a fizz, the cocktail should be left to settle for at least a minute before topping off with what remains in the shaker. Ideally, during the settling period, the glass should be placed in a glass froster/fridge/freezer. As the final contents of the shaker are poured into the glass so the head should rise like a souffle.

recipes.name: Ol' Reliable Daiquiri (straight-up)
recipes.instructions: Add 3/4 oz squeezed lime to a shaker. Go thin on this if you're making it for sweet types. Add 3/4 of simple 1-1 to preferably the same shaker. Add a thick 2 oz of whatever rum you have as long as it's not flavored or spiced. Make it Cuban aged rum if you're making it for someone pretty. Add ice cubes. If you've got a big cube, toss that in there with all the rest of 'em. Shake it till it's cold. Strain into a Nick & Nora if you want to impress someone. Strain into a double rocks if it's for yourself. If you're on a boat, strain into whatever and add cubes.

recipes.name: Fairytale of New York
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients (excluding the Ruby Port) into the shaker, and do a dry shake, to aerate the egg white. Do this for as long as you see fit - the longer the shake, the better the foam. Once you're happy with your dry shake, add ice to the tin and shake for approximately 10 seconds. Please note, if you're using a 1:1 sugar syrup, shake for approximately 6 to 8 seconds, as there is already more dilution. Once you've shaken, double strain into your chilled coupe. Once you've strained the drink and the foam on your drink has settled, float the 7.5ml of Ruby Port into your drink.

recipes.instructions: Pour the ingredients (excluding the Absinthe) into the shaker and shake for 8 seconds. Before straining, do two dashes of absinthe into the tea cup and swirl around the vessel, making sure it is fully coated. Then, discard the absinthe from the tea-cup and add a piece of hand-cut ice. Double strain the cocktail over the piece into the tea cup and present the vessel on a tea coaster.

recipes.name: Mark's Chicago Fizz
recipes.instructions:
SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and slowly pour 2/3rds of the contents of the shaker from a higher than normal height into a chilled empty glass (no ice in glass) while simultaneously pouring soda with your other hand. The cocktail and soda should form a single stream of liquid falling into the glass.

As when pouring a Guinness, to achieve a perfect head on a fizz, the cocktail should be left to settle for at least a minute before topping off with what remains in the shaker. Ideally, during the settling period, the glass should be placed in a glass froster/fridge/freezer. As the final contents of the shaker are poured into the glass so the head should rise like a souffle.

recipes.name: Caipiroska
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut a ripe lime in half (from pole to pole) and remove the core from each half by cutting a v-shaped grove. You'll need one-half of one large lime (or a whole small one) per drink and this should be chopped into wedges and each wedge chopped in half before dropping the resulting 6 or 8 lime chunks into a rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons powdered sugar and muddle. Not much.

3. When the limes's juice is extracted and the sugar dissolved, fill the glass with ice cubes.

4. Pour your favorite vodka into the glass. Stir to mix the ingredients.

5. Serve with a short straw.

recipes.name: The SmokeBombe
recipes.garnish: Halen Môn Oak Smoked Sugar rim Optional orange wedge
recipes.instructions:
x1 shot single grain whisky
x1 shot cognac
x1 teaspoon honey
x1 shot fresh squeezed lemon juice
2 dash Angostura bitters
x1 shot chilled water
for garnish: Halen Môn Sea Salt Oak Smoked Sugar, orange wedge.

Run cut lemon round the edge of a glass and dip into the smoked sugar. Fill glass with ice.

In a shaker, stir to dissolve honey into the whisky and cognac. Add remaining ingredients and pour into glass (careful not to disturb the rim). Garnish with optional orange wedge.

recipes.name: The Centre of the Galaxy
recipes.garnish: Garnish with a rim sugar made with equal quantities of freeze-dried raspberry powder with fine sugar.
recipes.instructions:
First make a raspberry syrup by mixing 100-125g (per cocktail) of fresh raspberries with a scant tablespoon of Demerara sugar. Add a splash of water and warm over a low heat in a small saucepan. Gently break up the mixture so that it becomes soupy and then pass through a fine sieve and then a muslin cloth to yield a clear syrup. Add more Demerara sugar to taste but you want it to be quite sour so don't overdo the sweetness.

Mix cooled raspberry syrup with the other ingredients and shake with ice until very cold - pour into a coupe glass or split into two nick and noras to share.

recipes.name: Dave's Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut a ripe lime in half (from pole to pole) and remove the core from each half by cutting a cut a v-shaped grove. You'll need one-half of the lime per drink and this should be chopped into wedges and each wedge chopped in half before dropping the resulting 6 or 8 lime chunks into the base tin of a shaker.

2. Add 3 bar spoons powdered sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. If using cubed ice straight from the freezer, add chilled water (or optionally add fruit of choice) and MUDDLE some more. If using watery ice then omit some or all of the water/juice.

4. When sure the limes's juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, POUR the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the shaker tin used to muddle and swirl to rinse tin before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to mix with other ingredients.

6. If using cubed ice, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Channing's Apple Daiquiri
recipes.instructions:
Take a plate and dust the cinnamon powder and caster sugar. Mix the two completely so that the cinnamon and sugar are distributed evenly throughout.

Cut a piece of lemon and rub around the entire rim of a glass you wish to serve in, alternatively use water instead of lemon juice.

Upturn the glass that you have just applied lemon juice/water to and dip in cinnamon sugar mix on the plate, you may need to tap around the edges of the glass in several motions. Continue till the entire rim is lightly covered in the cinnamon sugar mix.

SHAKE Apple Juice, Lemon Juice, White Rum and Sugar Syrup with ice and fine strain into prepared glass and SERVE with an apple wedge or plain.

recipes.name: Amanda's Strawberry Caipirinha
recipes.garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add chopped strawberries and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).

recipes.name: Paloma Faith'd

recipes.instructions:
To make drink
Add Olmeca, Pamplemousse Rose liqueur,dash of Peychauds And 25ml of the cordial (see below) top with soda.

Ingredient
Acid adjusted Passion fruit and Yuzu Cordial

To make 1ltr
Zest 3 Yuzu into 500gms powered sugar steep overnight then add Yuzu juice.
Remove pulp from 10 passion fruit add to Yuzu Sherbet. Sous vide for 3hrs
Add 10gms malic acid. (Malic is chosen as the Agave plant produces this at night.)
Fine strain and bottle
Keep in the fridge for 2weeks.

Stir slightly and garnish with a spiralled cucumber skin and a red cherry. The cucumber provides zest and looks attractive. This drink, created by Timo Haimi, took first prize in the 1991 Finlandia Vodka Long Drink Competition.

recipes.name: Almeria
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Shake well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Abilene
recipes.instructions: Pour all of the ingredients into a highball glass almost filled with ice cubes. Stir well.

recipes.name: Applecar
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Artillery
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Addington
recipes.instructions: Mix both the vermouth's in a shaker and strain into a cold glass. Top up with a squirt of Soda Water.

recipes.name: Bellini
recipes.instructions: Pour peach purée into chilled flute, add sparkling wine. Stir gently.

recipes.name: Balmoral
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Bluebird
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with crushed ice, combine the gin, triple sec, Curacao, and bitters. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with the lemon twist and the cherry.

recipes.name: Boomerang
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine the gin, vermouth, bitters, and maraschino liqueur. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with the cherry.

recipes.name: Brigadier
recipes.instructions: Mix ingredients in a warmed mug and stir.

recipes.name: Broadside
recipes.instructions: Half fill the glass with ice cubes. Crush the wormwood and add to ice. Pour rum, scotch and butters, then serve!

recipes.name: Blackthorn
recipes.instructions: Stir sloe gin and vermouth with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Add the twist of lemon peel and serve.

recipes.name: Bible Belt
recipes.instructions: Mix all ingredients, and pour over ice.

recipes.name: Baby Eskimo
recipes.instructions: Leave ice-cream out for about 10 minutes. Add ingredients in order, stir with chopstick (butter knife or spoon works too). Consume immediately and often. Nice and light, great for following a heavy drink.

recipes.name: Bahama Mama
recipes.instructions:
Add 2 parts club soda or more or less to taste.

Mix it all together and pour over a bunch of ice. Drink with a straw.

recipes.name: Cream Soda
recipes.instructions: Pour 1oz of Spiced Rum into a highball glass with ice. Fill with Ginger Ale.

recipes.name: Cuba Libra
recipes.instructions: Fill tall glass with ice cubes. Add rum. Rub cut edge of lime on rim of glass then squeeze juice into glass. Fill with Coca-Cola. Garnish with lime slice. Enjoy!

recipes.name: Cherry Rum
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Citrus Coke
recipes.instructions: Pour half of coke in a glass. Then add Bacardi and top it off with the remaining coke. Stir and drink up!

recipes.name: Casa Blanca
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: City Slicker
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Shake well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Casino Royale
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Shake well. Strain into a sour glass.

recipes.name: Corpse Reviver
recipes.instructions: Shake, strain, straight up, cocktail glass rinsed with absinthe

recipes.name: Clove Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Coffee Liqueur
recipes.instructions: Combine coffee, sugar and water. Simmer 1 hour and let cool. Add vanilla and vodka. Age in sealed jar 2 to 3 weeks.

recipes.name: Dragonfly
recipes.instructions: In a highball glass almost filled with ice cubes, combine the gin and ginger ale. Stir well. Garnish with the lime wedge.

recipes.name: Dry Rob Roy
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine the Scotch and vermouth. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with the lemon twist.

recipes.name: Darkwood Sling
recipes.instructions: There are many good cherry liqueurs you can use, but I prefere Heering. Add one share of the liqueur. Then you add one share of Soda. For a sour sling use Tonic (most people prefer the drink without Tonic). Afterwards you fill the glass with Orange Juice and ice cubes.

recipes.name: Dark and Stormy
recipes.instructions: In a highball glass filled with ice add 6cl dark rum and top with ginger beer. Garnish with lime wedge.

recipes.name: Dark Caipirinha
recipes.instructions:
Muddle the sugar into the lime wedges in an old-fashioned glass.
Fill the glass with ice cubes.
Pour the cachaca into the glass.
Stir well.

recipes.name: Dubonnet Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients (except lemon peel) with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Add the twist of lemon peel and serve.

recipes.name: English Highball
recipes.instructions: Pour brandy, gin, and sweet vermouth into a highball glass over ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water. Add the twist of lemon peel, stir, and serve. (Ginger ale may be substituted for carbonated water, if preferred.)

recipes.name: English Rose Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Rub rim of cocktail glass with lemon juice and dip rim of glass in powdered sugar. Shake all ingredients (except cherry) with ice and strain into sugar-rimmed glass. Top with the cherry and serve.

recipes.name: Elderflower Caipirinha
recipes.instructions: Take the glass and muddle the lime in it. Fill the glass with crushed ice and add the Cachaca. Stir well and top with some more crushed ice. Garnish with lime and enjoy!

recipes.name: Egg-Nog - Classic Cooked
recipes.instructions: In large saucepan, beat together eggs, sugar and salt, if desired. Stir in 2 cups of the milk. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until mixture is thick enough to coat a metal spoon and reaches 160 degrees F. Remove from heat. Stir in remaining 2 cups milk and vanilla. Cover and regfigerate until thoroughly chilled, several hours or overnight. Just before serving, pour into bowl or pitcher. Garnish or add stir-ins, if desired. Choose 1 or several of: Chocolate curls, cinnamon sticks, extracts of flavorings, flavored brandy or liqueur, fruit juice or nectar, ground nutmeg, maraschino cherries, orange slices, peppermint sticks or candy canes, plain brandy, run or whiskey, sherbet or ice-cream, whipping cream, whipped. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Empellón Cocina's Fat-Washed Mezcal
recipes.instructions:
To ensure that your pork fat is just as delicious as theirs, here’s their adobo marinade and what to do with it (you’ll also need a rack of ribs):

4 ancho chiles, 8 guajillo chiles and 4 chipotle chiles, plus 4 cloves roasted garlic, half a cup of cider vinegar, a quarter teaspoon of Mexican oregano, 1 teaspoon of ground black pepper, a whole clove, a quarter teaspoon of ground cinnamon and a half teaspoon of ground cumin.

Toast the dried chiles and soak in water for at least an hour until they are rehydrated. Drain and discard the soaking liquid. Combine the soaked chiles with the remaining ingredients and purée until smooth.

Cold smoke a rack of baby back pork ribs by taking a large hotel pan with woodchips on one side and charcoal on the other. Place another, smaller, pan with pork ribs, above the charcoal/woodchip pan. Ignite the charcoal, being careful to not ignite the woodchips. Cover both pans with foil and allow to smoke for 10-15 minutes, until desired level of smoke is achieved, then coat with adobo marinade and wrap in tin foil prior to placing ribs in a 300 degree oven for 7 hours. When the ribs have cooled, strain off the fat and use for the infusion.

If you’re having a hard time coming up to the same kind of volume of fat, make up the balance with pork lard from a butcher. To get the same depth of flavor without the ribs, heat up the fat in a pot with a few spoons of the marinade.

Once you’ve got your tub of seasoned pork fat in cooled liquid form, pour equal amounts of Ilegal Joven mezcal and fat into a sealable container. Seal the container and give it a really good shake, then put it in the freezer overnight. When the whole thing is separated and congealed, pour it through a fine mesh chinoise. If you don’t have a chinoise, try a fine mesh strainer, or if you don’t have one of those, try spooning off most of the fat. There will be some beads of orange fat left in the strained mezcal: run that through a few layers of cheesecloth (or coffee filters in a pinch) to get rid of the last of it.

The mezcal is now ready for drinking, straight-up or in a cocktail. 

Habanero tincture

Slice habaneros and add 2 ounces Ilegal Joven mezcal.
Allow to sit overnight or until desired level of heat is achieved.
Cocktail

Combine mezcal and chocolate liqueur in a mixing glass with ice and stir for 45 seconds.
Strain into chilled coupe.
Carefully "sink" the coffee liqueur down the inside of the coupe over a spoon.
Garnish with 5 drops habanero tincture.

recipes.name: Funk and Soul
recipes.instructions: Mix all ingredients together and strain into a Collins glass. Use Jamaican rum where possible for a more authentic taste.

recipes.name: French Negroni
recipes.instructions:
Add ice to a shaker and pour in all ingredients.
Using a bar spoon, stir 40 to 45 revolutions or until thoroughly chilled.
Strain into a martini glass or over ice into a rocks glass. Garnish with orange twist.

recipes.name: Frozen Daiquiri
recipes.instructions: Combine all ingredients (except for the cherry) in an electric blender and blend at a low speed for five seconds, then blend at a high speed until firm. Pour contents into a champagne flute, top with the cherry, and serve.

recipes.name: Flying Scotchman
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Flaming Lamborghini
recipes.instructions: Pour the Sambuca and Kahlua into the Cocktail Glass and give the drinker a straw. Pour the Baileys and Blue Curacao into two sepsrate shot glasses either side of the cocktail glass. Set light the concotion in the cocktail glass and start to drink through the straw (this drink should be drunk in one) , as the bottom of the glass is reached put out the fire by pouring the Baileys and Blue Curacao into the cocktail glass and keep drinking till it's all gone!!

recipes.name: Frozen Mint Daiquiri
recipes.instructions: Combine all ingredients with 1 cup of crushed ice in an electric blender. Blend at a low speed for a short length of time. Pour into an old-fashioned glass and serve.

recipes.name: Frozen Pineapple Daiquiri
recipes.instructions: Combine all ingredients with 1 cup of crushed ice in an electric blender. Blend at a low speed for a short length of time. Pour into a cocktail glass and serve.

recipes.name: GG
recipes.instructions: Pour the Galliano liqueur over ice. Fill the remainder of the glass with ginger ale and thats all there is to it. You now have a your very own GG.

recipes.name: Godfather
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients directly into old fashioned glass filled with ice cubes. Stir gently.

recipes.name: Gluehwein
recipes.instructions: Boil sugar and spices in water, leave in the water for 30 minutes. Strain the spiced water and mix with the wine. Heat slowly until short of boiling temperature. (To remove alcohol, let it boil for a while.) You may add lemon or orange juice to taste. Serve in irish coffee cup.

recipes.name: Gin Tonic
recipes.instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice, pour the gin, top with tonic water and squeeze a lemon wedge and garnish with a lemon wedge.

recipes.name: Gin Toddy
recipes.instructions: Mix powdered sugar and water in an old-fashioned glass. Add gin and one ice cube. Stir, add the twist of lemon peel, and serve.

recipes.name: Gin Smash
recipes.instructions: Muddle sugar with carbonated water and mint sprigs in an old-fashioned glass. Add gin and 1 ice cube. Stir, add the orange slice and the cherry, and serve.

recipes.name: Gin Lemon
recipes.instructions: For the preparation of the gin lemon you will not need the shaker. Fill the tumbler with ice, pour the gin and lemonade over it. Gently mix and decorate with a slice of lemon. Those who prefer can also add a few mint leaves. Your gin lemon is ready to be served.

recipes.name: Greyhound
recipes.instructions:
Add the vodka to a Collins glass filled with ice.
Top with grapefruit juice and stir.

recipes.name: Gin Squirt
recipes.instructions: Stir gin, grenadine, and powdered sugar with ice and strain into a highball glass over ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water and stir. Decorate with the pineapple chunks and the strawberries and serve.

recipes.name: Grand Blue
recipes.instructions: Serve in an old fashioned glass.

recipes.name: Gin Cooler
recipes.instructions: Stir powdered sugar and 2 oz. carbonated water in a collins glass. Fill glass with ice and add gin. Fill with carbonated water and stir. Add the lemon peel and the orange spiral so that the end of the orange spiral dangles over rim of glass.

recipes.name: Gin Swizzle
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine the lime juice, sugar, gin, and bitters. Shake well. Almost fill a colling glass with ice cubes. Stir until the glass is frosted. Strain the mixture in the shaker into the glass and add the club soda.

recipes.name: Grass Skirt
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine the gin, triple sec, pineapple juice, and grenadine. Shake well. Pour into an old-fashioned glass and garnish with the pineapple slice.

recipes.name: Grim Reaper
recipes.instructions: Mix Kahlua and 151 in glass. Quickly add ice and pour grenadine over ice to give ice red tint.

recipes.name: Gin and Soda
recipes.instructions:
Pour the Gin and Soda water into a highball glass almost filled with ice cubes.
Stir well.
Garnish with the lime wedge.

recipes.name: Hot Toddy
recipes.instructions:
STEP 1
Whisk the whisky and honey together and split between 2 heatproof glasses. Add half of the cinnamon stick to each, then top up with 200ml boiling water.

STEP 2
Add a splash of lemon juice to each, then taste and add more to your preference. Finish each with a slice of lemon, studded with a clove, and serve immediately.

recipes.name: Horse's Neck
recipes.instructions: Pour brandy and ginger ale directly into highball glass with ice cubes. Stir gently. Garnish with lemon zest. If desired, add dashes of Angostura Bitter.

recipes.name: Happy Skipper
recipes.instructions: Pour Captain Morgan's Spiced Rum over ice, fill glass to top with Ginger Ale. Garnish with lime. Tastes like a cream soda. Named for the Gilligan's Island reference ("The Captain" *in* "Ginger" is a Happy Skipper!)

recipes.name: Hunter's Moon
recipes.instructions: Put the Bombay Sapphire, Martini Bianco, sugar syrup & blackberries in a cocktail shaker with lots of ice and shake vigorously before pouring into a balloon glass, topping up with lemonade and garnishing with a wedge of orange.

recipes.name: Havana Cocktail
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Shake well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Hemingway Special
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients into a shaker with ice. Shake.

recipes.name: Highland Fling Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients (except olive) with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Add the olive and serve.

recipes.name: Irish Spring
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients (except orange slice and cherry) into a collins glass over ice cubes. Garnish with the slice of orange, add the cherry on top, and serve.

recipes.name: Imperial Fizz
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except carbonated water) with ice and strain into a highball glass over two ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Imperial Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Shake with ice and strain into cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Irish Curdling Cow
recipes.instructions: Pour Irish Cream, Vodka, and Bourbon in a glass. Add some ice and mix in the orange juice.

recipes.name: Jitterbug
recipes.instructions: Wet glass, dip rim in sugar. Then add Ice. Then add everything else. It's that simple!

recipes.name: Jackhammer
recipes.instructions: Serve over ice- Warning,Deadly!

recipes.name: Jamaica Kiss
recipes.instructions: Fill a tumbler with ice cubes. Add a shot of Tia Maria and a shot of Jamaican light rum. Fill the tumbler with milk. Blend until smooth and serve immediately.

recipes.name: Japanese Fizz
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except carbonated water) with ice and strain into a highball glass over two ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Jewel Of The Nile
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Jack Rose Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Jack's Vanilla Coke
recipes.instructions: After pouring in your ingredients, and adding 3-5 ice cubes, according to taste. Stir the drink with a stirrer to get the Vanilla off the bottom.

recipes.name: Kir
recipes.instructions: Add the crème de cassis to the bottom of the glass, then top up with wine.

recipes.name: Karsk
recipes.instructions: Put a copper coin in a coffe-cup and fill up with coffee until you no longer see the coin, then add alcohol until you see the coin. Norwegian speciality.

recipes.name: Kir Royale
recipes.instructions: Pour Creme de cassis in glass, gently pour champagne on top

recipes.name: Kiwi Lemon
recipes.instructions: Mix in highball glass. Stirr. Garnish with slice of kiwi.

recipes.name: Kentucky B And B
recipes.instructions: Pour the bourbon and Benedictine into a brandy snifter.

recipes.name: Long vodka
recipes.instructions: Shake a tall glass with ice cubes and Angostura, coating the inside of the glass. Por the vodka onto this, add 1 slice of lime and squeeze juice out of remainder, mix with tonic, stir and voila you have a Long Vodka

recipes.name: Loch Lomond
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine the Scotch, Drambuie, and vermouth. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with the lemon twist.

recipes.name: London Town
recipes.instructions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Lady Love Fizz
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except carbonated water) with ice and strain into a cocktail glass over two ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Long Island Tea
recipes.instructions: Combine all ingredients (except cola) and pour over ice in a highball glass. Add the splash of cola for color. Decorate with a slice of lemon and serve.

recipes.name: Lone Tree Cooler
recipes.instructions: Stir powdered sugar and 2 oz. carbonated water in a collins glass. Fill glass with ice, add gin and vermouth, and stir. Fill with carbonated water and stir again. Add the twist of lemon peel and the orange spiral so that the end dangles over rim of glass.

recipes.name: Lone Tree Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Lazy Coconut Paloma
recipes.instructions: Mix the coconut liqueur (preferably tequila) with the grapefruit juice and top with soda water. Garnish with a large grapefruit slice against the inside of the glass.

recipes.name: Long Island Iced Tea
recipes.instructions: Mix all contents in a highball glass and sitr gently. Add dash of Coca-Cola for the coloring and garnish with lemon or lime twist.

recipes.name: Lemon Elderflower Spritzer
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients over ice, stir and enjoy!

recipes.name: Mimosa
recipes.instructions: Ensure both ingredients are well chilled, then mix into the glass. Serve cold.

recipes.name: Martini
recipes.instructions: Straight: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice cubes. Stir well. Strain in chilled martini cocktail glass. Squeeze oil from lemon peel onto the drink, or garnish with olive.

recipes.name: Mauresque
recipes.instructions:
1 - Pour the Ricard (or pastis)
2 - Pour the orgeat syrup
3 - Finally pour the water and add ice cubes at your convenience.

Add the ice cubes at the end, otherwise the syrup and pastis do not mix well.

recipes.name: Mudslinger
recipes.instructions: Add all contents to a large jug or punch bowl. Stir well!

recipes.name: Martinez 2
recipes.instructions:
Add all ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice.

Stir until chilled, and strain into a chilled coupe glass.

recipes.name: Mulled Wine
recipes.instructions: Simmer 3 cups water with, sugar, cloves, cinnamon sticks, and lemon peel in a stainless steel pot for 10 minutes. Add wine heat to a "coffee temperature" (DO NOT BOIL) then add the brandy.

recipes.name: Munich Mule
recipes.instructions:
Fill glass with ice
Pour Gin and Lime Juice
Fill glass with Ginger Beer
Garnish with Cucumer and Lime slice

recipes.name: Mojito Extra
recipes.instructions: Put mint with lemon juice in a glas, mash the mint with a spoon, ice, rum & fill up with club soda. Top it with Angostura.

recipes.name: Midnight Mint
recipes.instructions: If available, rim cocktail (Martini) glass with sugar syrup then dip into chocolate flakes or powder. Add ingredients into shaker with ice. Shake well then strain into cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Mary Pickford
recipes.instructions: Shake and strain into a chilled large cocktail glass

recipes.name: Monkey Wrench
recipes.instructions: Pour all of the ingredients into an old-fashioned glass almost filled with ice cubes. Stir well.

recipes.name: Nutty Irishman
recipes.instructions: Serve over ice

recipes.name: National Aquarium
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients into a shaker of ice. Shake well. Serve on the rocks.

recipes.name: New York Lemonade
recipes.instructions: Serve in a chilled cocktail glass. Lemon and sugar the rim. Stir and Strain.

recipes.name: Orgasm
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a chilled cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Orange Oasis
recipes.instructions: Shake brandy, gin, and orange juice with ice and strain into a highball glass over ice cubes. Fill with ginger ale, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Orange Push-up
recipes.instructions: Combine liquors in a blender. Add a half scoop of ice and blend. Garnish with an orange and cherry flag. So good it will melt in your mouth!!!

recipes.name: Owen's Grandmother's Revenge
recipes.instructions: Add ingredients and mix in blender.

recipes.name: Pegu Club
recipes.instructions: Shake, strain, up, cocktail glass

recipes.name: Pink Lady
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Pink Moon
recipes.instructions: Slowly shake in a shaker with ice, strain into a square whiskey glass. Top with fresh ice. Add the blackberries to garnish. Add flowers and a green leaf for a special look!

recipes.name: Porto flip
recipes.instructions: Shake ingredients together in a mixer with ice. Strain into glass, garnish and serve.

recipes.name: Pina Colada
recipes.instructions: Mix with crushed ice in blender until smooth. Pour into chilled glass, garnish and serve.

recipes.name: Pink Penocha
recipes.instructions: mix all ingredients into bowl keep iced stir frequently

recipes.name: Pure Passion
recipes.instructions: Mix up all ingredients with a cocktail stirrer and serve with crushed ice with mint and edible flour if available.

recipes.name: Popped cherry
recipes.instructions: Served over ice in a tall glass with a popped cherry (can add more popped cherries if in the mood)!

recipes.name: Poppy Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Shake ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Port Wine Flip
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except nutmeg) with ice and strain into a whiskey sour glass. Sprinkle nutmeg on top and serve.

recipes.name: Pornstar Martini
recipes.instructions: Straight: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice cubes. Shake well. Strain in chilled martini cocktail glass. Cut passion fruit in half and use as garnish. Pour prosecco into a chilled shot glass and serve alongside the martini.

recipes.name: Planter’s Punch
recipes.instructions: Squeeze an orange and strain the juice. Put all the ingredients in a shaker filled with ice and shake for at least 12 seconds. Strain into a highball glass and decorate with a pineapple wedge or fruit of your choice.

recipes.name: Port And Starboard
recipes.instructions: Pour carefully into a pousse-cafe glass, so that creme de menthe floats on grenadine. Serve without mixing.

recipes.name: Port Wine Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Passion Fruit Martini
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients into a glass and stir. Garnish with half a passion fruit piece.

recipes.name: Quentin
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine the rum, Kahlua, and cream. Shake well. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with the nutmeg.

recipes.name: Queen Bee
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Quick-sand
recipes.instructions: Simply add the orange juice, quite a quick pour in order to mix the sambucca with the orange juice. The juice MUST have fruit pulp!

recipes.name: Queen Charlotte
recipes.instructions: Pour red wine and grenadine into a collins glass over ice cubes. Fill with lemon-lime soda, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Queen Elizabeth
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Quaker's Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Quarter Deck Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Rose
recipes.instructions: Shake together in a cocktail shaker, then strain into chilled glass. Garnish and serve.

recipes.name: Radler
recipes.instructions: Pour beer into large mug, slowly add the 7-up (or Sprite).

recipes.name: Rum Toddy
recipes.instructions: Dissolve powdered sugar in water in an old-fashioned glass. Add rum and one ice cube and stir. Add the twist of lemon peel and serve.

recipes.name: Royal Fizz
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except cola) with ice and strain into a chilled collins glass. Fill with cola and serve.

recipes.name: Rum Cooler
recipes.instructions: Pour the rum and soda into a collins glass almost filled with ice cubes. Stir well and garnish with the lemon wedge.

recipes.name: Rum Runner
recipes.instructions: Mix all ingredients in glass & add ice.

recipes.name: Rusty Nail
recipes.instructions: Pour the Scotch and Drambuie into an old-fashioned glass almost filled with ice cubes. Stir well. Garnish with the lemon twist.

recipes.name: Rum Cobbler
recipes.instructions: In an old-fashioned glass, dissolve the sugar in the club soda. Add crushed ice until the glass is almost full. Add the rum. Stir well. Garnish with the cherry and the orange and lemon slices.

recipes.name: Ruby Tuesday
recipes.instructions: Pour gin and cranberry into a highball filled with ice cubes. Add grenadine and stir.

recipes.name: Rosemary Blue
recipes.instructions:
1) Add the Bombay Sapphire, Blue Curacao, rosemary sprig and gently squeezed lemon wedge to a balloon glass. Swirl well to combine.
2) Fill with cubed ice and top with the Fever-Tree Light Tonic Water.
3) Gently fold with a bar spoon to mix.

recipes.name: Ramos Gin Fizz
recipes.instructions:
Prepare all the ingredients on the counter to be able to work well and quickly, especially the cream and egg white.

Pour all the ingredients into a shaker.

Shake vigorously for 1 minute: cream and egg white must be mixed perfectly, so don't rush.

Now open the shaker and put some ice and shake for 1-2 minutes. It depends on how long you can resist!

Pour into a highball glass, add a splash of soda and garnish to taste.

Ramos Gin Fizz was once drunk as an invigorating drink or even as a breakfast, try it as an aperitif and after dinner and you will discover a little gem now lost.

recipes.name: Royal Gin Fizz
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except carbonated water) with ice and strain into a highball glass over two ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Rum Milk Punch
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except nutmeg) with ice and strain into a collins glass. Sprinkle nutmeg on top and serve.

recipes.name: Raspberry Julep
recipes.instructions: Softly muddle the mint leaves and raspberry syrup in the bottom of the cup. Add crushed ice and Bourbon to the cup and then stir. Top with more ice, garnish with a mint sprig.

recipes.name: Rum Screwdriver
recipes.instructions: Pour rum into a highball glass over ice cubes. Add orange juice, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Raspberry Cooler
recipes.instructions: Pour the raspberry vodka and soda into a highball glass almost filled with ice cubes. Stir well.

recipes.name: Rum Old-fashioned
recipes.instructions: Stir powdered sugar, water, and bitters in an old-fashioned glass. When sugar has dissolved add ice cubes and light rum. Add the twist of lime peel, float 151 proof rum on top, and serve.

recipes.name: Russian Spring Punch
recipes.instructions: Pour the ingredients into an highball glass, top with Sparkling wine.

recipes.name: Radioactive Long Island Iced Tea
recipes.instructions: Pour all ingredients over ice in a very tall glass. Sip cautiously.

recipes.name: Smut
recipes.instructions: Throw it all together and serve real cold.

recipes.name: Spritz
recipes.instructions: Build into glass over ice, garnish and serve.

recipes.name: Scooter
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients well with cracked ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Sangria
recipes.instructions: Mix all together in a pitcher and refrigerate. Add cloves and cinnamon sticks to taste. Serve in wine glasses.

recipes.name: Snowball
recipes.instructions: Place one ice cube in the glass and add 1 1/2 oz of Advocaat. Fill up the glass with lemonade and decorate with a slice of lemon. Serve at once.

recipes.name: Sweet Tooth
recipes.instructions: Put 2 shots Godiva Liquour into a glass, add as much or as little milk as you would like.

recipes.name: Screwdriver
recipes.instructions: Mix in a highball glass with ice. Garnish and serve.

recipes.name: Sherry Flip
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients (except nutmeg) with ice and strain into a whiskey sour glass. Sprinkle nutmeg on top and serve.

recipes.name: Sol Y Sombra
recipes.instructions: Shake ingredients with ice, strain into a brandy snifter, and serve. (The English translation of the name of this drink is "Sun and Shade", and after sampling this drink, you'll understand why. Thanks, Kirby.)

recipes.name: Shark Attack
recipes.instructions: Mix lemonade and water according to instructions on back of can. If the instructions say to add 4 1/3 cans of water do so. Mix into pitcher. Add 1 1/2 cup of Vodka (Absolut). Mix well. Pour into glass of crushed ice. Excellent!

recipes.name: Sherry Eggnog
recipes.instructions: Shake sherry, powdered sugar, and egg with ice and strain into a collins glass. Fill with milk and stir. Sprinkle nutmeg on top and serve.

recipes.name: Thriller
recipes.instructions: In a shaker half-filled with ice cubes, combine all of the ingredients. Shake well. Strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: The Galah
recipes.instructions: Mix together the alcoholic portions and top with Pineapple and Lime juice.

recipes.name: Tia-Maria
recipes.instructions: Boil water, sugar and coffe for 10 mins and let cool. Add rum and vanilla. Put in clean bottle(s) and leave for 1 week before using.

recipes.name: Tipperary
recipes.instructions: Stir over ice. Strain into chilled glass. Cut a wide swath of orange peel, and express the orange oils over the drink. Discard orange twist.

recipes.name: Texas Sling
recipes.instructions: Blend with Ice until smooth. Serve in a tulip glass, top with whip cream.

recipes.name: The Last Word
recipes.instructions: Shake with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

recipes.name: Turf Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients (except orange peel) with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Add the twist of orange peel and serve.

recipes.name: The Laverstoke
recipes.instructions:
1) Squeeze two lime wedges into a balloon glass then add the cordial, Bombay Sapphire and MARTINI Rosso Vermouth, swirl to mix.

2) Fully fill the glass with cubed ice and stir to chill.

3) Top with Fever-Tree Ginger Ale and gently stir again to combine.

4) Garnish with a snapped ginger slice and an awoken mint sprig.

recipes.name: The Philosopher
recipes.instructions: Add all the spirits in a shaker (best to use Hendricks gin) as well as the orange bitters and lemon juice. Strain into a Margarita glass, top with Prosecco.

recipes.name: Tuxedo Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry and a twist of lemon zest.

recipes.name: The Jimmy Conway
recipes.instructions:
Fill glass with ice
Pour in The Irishman and Disaronno
Fill to the top with Cranberry Juice
Garnish with a slice of lemon…Enjoy!

recipes.name: Victor
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Vampiro
recipes.instructions: Vampiros may be made in a tall glass or an old fashioned glass. Bartenders may first "rim" the glass with Kosher Salt, which is done by placing a layer of Kosher Salt on a chopping board, moistening the glass' rim with lime juice or water, and then placing the upside down glass rim onto the Kosher Salt, so that the salt sticks to the moistened rim. The second step is to fill half the glass with ice and add one or two shooter glasses full of high quality Tequila. The next stage is to add the flavouring elements. This is done by squeezing a fresh lime into the glass, adding a few grains of salt, adding citrus-flavoured soda pop, until the glass is 4/5 full, and then adding spicy Viuda de Sanchez (or orange juice, lime juice and pico de gallo). The final step is to stir the ingredients so that the flavours are properly blended.

recipes.name: Vesuvio
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into an old-fashioned glass over ice cubes, and serve.

recipes.name: Veteran
recipes.instructions: Pour the rum and cherry brandy into an old-fashioned glass almost filled with ice cubes. Stir well.

recipes.name: Van Vleet
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into an old-fashioned glass over ice cubes, and serve.

recipes.name: Vodka Lemon
recipes.instructions: The vodka lemon is prepared directly in a highball glass or in a large tumbler: put 6-7 ice cubes in the glass, pour the vodka, lemonade and mix with a bar spoon. Finally decorate with a slice of lemon and, if you prefer, add a few mint leaves. Your vodka lemon is ready to be served.

recipes.name: Vodka Slime
recipes.instructions: Fill glass with ice. Add vodka, 7-up then finish with the lime juice.

recipes.name: Vodka Tonic
recipes.instructions:
Wash and cut 1 wedge and 1 slice of lime or lemon.
Fill a tumbler with fresh ice.
Pour the desired dose of vodka and top up with the tonic.
Squeeze the lime wedge into the glass and decorate with the slice.
That's all, very simple: it's just the recipe for happiness!

recipes.name: Vodka Martini
recipes.instructions: Shake the vodka and vermouth together with a number of ice cubes, strain into a cocktail glass, add the olive and serve.

recipes.name: Vodka Russian
recipes.instructions: Mix it as a ordinary drink .

recipes.name: Vermouth Cassis
recipes.instructions: Stir vermouth and creme de cassis in a highball glass with ice cubes. Fill with carbonated water, stir again, and serve.

recipes.name: Vodka And Tonic
recipes.instructions: Pour vodka into a highball glass over ice cubes. Fill with tonic water, stir, and serve.

recipes.name: Valencia Cocktail
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Whisky Mac
recipes.instructions: Pour both of the ingredients into a wine goblet with no ice.

recipes.name: Wine Punch
recipes.instructions: Combine all of the ingredients and pour over a block of ice.

recipes.name: Wine Cooler
recipes.instructions: Mix wine and soft drink. Pour into glass. Add ice.

recipes.name: Winter Paloma
recipes.instructions: Everyone’s favourite Paloma gets a delicious Indian makeover. Tequila reposado infused with “Timur Pepper” which has citrusy & grapefruit notes and is grown at the foothills of Himalaya. It also produces a slightly numbing and tingling sensation on your lip when consumed. We have also spiced up the fresh grapefruit juice with the warming spice blend from Himalaya. The combination of all these interesting elements has allowed us to elevate your Paloma sipping experience.

recipes.name: White Wine Sangria
recipes.instructions: Chop the Lemon, Lime and other fruits into large chunks. Fill the Pitcher with the white wine and mix in the Apple Brandy. Top to taste with soda water.

recipes.name: Whitecap Margarita
recipes.instructions: Place all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth. This makes one drink.

recipes.name: Waikiki Beachcomber
recipes.instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

recipes.name: Yellow Bird
recipes.instructions: Shake and strain into a chilled cocktail glass

recipes.name: Zambeer
recipes.instructions: Mix sambuca with rootbeer and stir. Add ice

recipes.name: Zenmeister
recipes.instructions: Mix together and enjoy

recipes.name: Zima Blaster
recipes.instructions: Fill glass with ice. Pour in Chambord, then fill with Zima. Mix and enjoy.

recipes.name: Zimadori Zinger
recipes.instructions: Pour Zima in a collins glass over ice and then pour the shot of Midori. Don't stir. Garnish with a cherry.

recipes.name: Ziemes Martini Apfelsaft
recipes.instructions: Serve without ice. At least the juice shold have room temperature.

recipes.name: 155 Belmont
recipes.instructions: Blend with ice. Serve in a wine glass. Garnish with carrot.

recipes.name: 410 Gone
recipes.instructions: Mix together and enjoy!
fill glass with crushed ice. Add vodka. Add a splash of grand-marnier. Fill with o.j.

recipes.name: 501 Blue
recipes.instructions: Mix equal amounts into a glass with ice.

recipes.name: 57 Chevy with a White License Plate
recipes.instructions: 1. Fill a rocks glass with ice 2.add white creme de cacao and vodka 3.stir

recipes.name: 69 Special
recipes.instructions: Pour 2 oz. gin. Add 4 oz. 7-up. Add Lemon Juice for flavor. If you are weak, top up glass with more 7-Up.

recipes.name: 747 Drink
recipes.instructions:
Fill a Collins glass with ice.
Pour in vodka, lime cordial, and cranberry juice.
Fill up with Sprite.
Garnish with a Lime wheel or some cranberries

recipes.name: 9 1/2 Weeks
recipes.instructions: Combine all ingredients in glass mixer. Chill and strain into Cocktail glass. Garnish with sliced strawberry.
